metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2451
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Myc-LRRTM4 induces clustering of endogenous GPC4 in coculture assays.
HA-GPC4 expressed in 293T cells induces clustering of the excitatory postsynaptic marker PSD-95 but not of the inhibitory postsynaptic marker gephyrin in coculture assays.
HA-GPC4-mediated clustering of endogenous LRRTM4 in contacting dendrites requires GAG chains in coculture assays.

21/2/2018 Pim
- Abstract: "Glypican binds LRRTM4, but not LRRTM2, in an HS-dependent manner. Glypican 4 (GPC4) and LRRTM4 localize to the pre- and postsynaptic membranes of excitatory synapses, respectively. Consistent with a trans-synaptic interaction, LRRTM4 triggers GPC4 clustering in contacting axons and GPC4 induces clustering of LRRTM4 in contacting dendrites in an HS-dependent manner. ". Experimental description: Experimental Procedures
Neuronal Cultures
Hippocampal neurons were cultured from P0 Long-Evans rats (Charles River) and plated on poly-D-lysine-coated (Millipore) and laminin-coated (Invitrogen) chamber slides.

Plasmids
Full-length cDNAs encoding mouse LRRTM4 (accession number AY182031), human LRRTM4 (BC110058), and mouse glypicans GPC1 (BC062902), GPC2 (BC083180), GPC3 (BC036126), GPC4 (BC006622), GPC5 (BC115712) and GPC6 (BC023448) were purchased from Open Biosystems/Thermo Scientific.
